messages.content:
You are a relevance classifier for a portfolio risk app.

For each article below, determine if it's relevant to any of the given positions.

Positions:
{positions}

Articles:
{articles}

Return JSON array where each entry has:
- "article_index": 0-based index
- "relevant": true/false
- "affected_tickers": ["NVDA", "AAPL"] (if relevant)
- "event_type": "company_specific|macro|sector|theme|irrelevant"
- "why_it_matters": one sentence

Rules:
- If article mentions a held ticker by name or alias → relevant (company_specific)
- If macro/financial news matches position themes → relevant (macro/sector)
- Analyst downgrades/upgrades with price targets → minor, still relevant
- General market commentary with no specific tickers → irrelevant unless macro theme matches
- Only flag articles as relevant if there's a clear connection to held positions
- Quote pages, chart pages, generic price pages, and low-information stock recaps are irrelevant even if they mention the ticker

Positions:
- ABBV: growth archetype
- ABT: growth archetype
- AOS: growth archetype
- MMM: growth archetype

Articles:
[0] Title: Treasury Secretary Bessent backs rate cuts but says he understands if the Fed wants to wait
    Summary: Bessent has previously said the Fed should hasten cutting interest rates, calling reductions "the only ingredient missing" for stronger economic growth.

[1] Title: David Einhorn signals caution as his hedge fund Greenlight prioritizes capital protection
    Summary: "It probably won't surprise anyone that we are again putting capital preservation at the top of our priorities," Einhorn said in his latest investor letter.

[2] Title: Global recession is inevitable if Strait of Hormuz stays shut, says Citadel's Ken Griffin
    Summary: A prolonged blockage of the Strait of Hormuz for the next six to 12 months will mean an unavoidable recession, the billionaire investor said.

[3] Title: CEOs are betting AI will augment work rather than displace all workers
    Summary: Panelists at the Semafor World Economy conference said artificial intelligence can supplement the work that's already done, improving its quantity and quality.

[4] Title: Amazon to buy Globalstar to bolster Leo satellite business in deal worth about $11.6 billion
    Summary: Amazon said it would acquire Globalstar, giving its nascent satellite internet business a boost as it vies to compete with Elon Musk's SpaceX.

response:
```json
[
  {
    "article_index": 0,
    "relevant": false,
    "affected_tickers": [],
    "event_type": "irrelevant",
    "why_it_matters": "General Fed/monetary policy commentary with no specific mentions of held positions."
  },
  {
    "article_index": 1,
    "relevant": false,
    "affected_tickers": [],
    "event_type": "irrelevant",
    "why_it_matters": "Hedge fund investor letter focused on capital preservation with no mentions of held tickers."
  },
  {
    "article_index": 2,
    "relevant": false,
    "affected_tickers": [],
    "event_type": "irrelevant",
    "why_it_matters": "Macro recession risk warning for Strait of Hormuz with no specific ticker mentions."
  },
  {
    "article_index": 3,
    "relevant": false,
    "affected_tickers": [],
    "event_type": "irrelevant",
    "why_it_matters": "General AI panel discussion at conference with no specific tickers or connection to held positions."
  },
  {
    "article_index": 4,
    "relevant": false,
    "affected_tickers": [],
    "event_type": "irrelevant",
    "why_it_matters": "M&A news for Amazon and Globalstar; neither ticker is in the portfolio."
  }
]
```
